sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत । स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  हे अच्युत  आपकी कृपासे मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया है और स्मृति प्राप्त हो गयी है ।  मैं सन्देहरहित होकर स्थित हूँ ।  अब मैं आपकी आज्ञाका पालन करूँगा ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna said: By your grace, O Achyuta, my delusion is destroyed and memory restored. I stand free of doubt and will do what you say.
essence: Grace ends confusion and turns insight into obedience.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says Krishna's grace has removed his confusion. His doubt is gone, and he is ready to follow Krishna's instruction.
simpleInsight: Relief comes when inner conflict ends and the next step becomes clear.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna has been shattered by grief and hesitation. After Krishna's teaching, he speaks with a changed mind: confusion has fallen away, clarity has returned, and he is ready to act in alignment with Krishna.
modernRelevance: You finally make the call after days of avoidance. The knot in your chest loosens, not because the choice became easy, but because you know what you must do.
translationLiteral: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / नष्टः (destroyed) / मोहः (delusion) / स्मृतिः (memory) / लब्धा (obtained) / त्वत्-प्रसादात् (by your grace) / मया (by me) / अच्युत (O Achyuta) / स्थितः अस्मि (I am standing) / गत-सन्देहः (without doubt) / करिष्ये (I will do) / वचनम् (word) / तव (your)
